note: All measured on the same Windows/MSVC box. Compiled tiers at -O2/release; CPython for Python. See benchmarks/README.md and benchmarks/crosslang/run_perf.ps1.

workloads.name: count_primes_below(300000)
workloads.unit: ms (whole-program wall time, best-of-9; Python best-of-1)
workloads.note: Lullaby uses the native `%` operator for the trial-division remainder (matching C's `n % d`); the prior `rem(a,b)=a-(a/b)*b` helper measured 1.04x.

workloads.name: fib(35) recursive
workloads.note: The residual gap is per-call overhead: C at /O2 keeps the argument in a callee-saved register across the two recursive calls; closing it needs tighter recursive-call codegen.

workloads.name: element-wise map c[i]=a[i]+b[i] (i64 array)
workloads.note: The native backend recognizes `for i: c[i] = a[i] +/- b[i]` over `array<i64>` and emits an SSE2 packed loop (movdqu/paddq/psubq/movdqu, two i64 lanes per iteration) with a scalar tail. Measured against the identical loop forced scalar (two-statement body defeats the matcher). Output is bit-for-bit identical to every interpreter (parity-tested).

optimization_history.note: Native backend progression this campaign (fib ns/call vs C, and loop ns/iter vs C).
